subject: Accounting
title: Revenue: The Top Line
description: A 60-second lesson on Revenue: The Top Line.

hook.text: The income statement starts at the top with one number: how much the company earned.

buildup.text: Revenue—also called sales or turnover—is the total money generated from selling goods or services.

discovery.text: It doesn't include borrowed money or investment gains—only core business income.

twist.text: Companies sometimes inflate revenue by counting future sales early. That's how Enron started.

climax.text: Revenue growth gets headlines, but it means nothing if costs eat it all.

punchline.text: Revenue is vanity. Profit is sanity.

quiz.question: What does revenue on the income statement represent?
options: Total sales from core business operations
